- central_treasury: Alpha-Custody=7pts, Beta-Custody=3pts, Gamma-Custody=2pts
- defense_command: Alpha-Command=6pts, Beta-Command=3pts, Gamma-Command=2pts
Your BATNA (no deal) is worth 6 points total.
Best possible deal: 22 points. A good deal is 16+ points.

BATNA WARNING: If no agreement is reached, you get only 6 points.
Any deal scoring above 6 is better than no deal. But don't settle
for barely above your BATNA — push for the best terms possible.

ENDGAME: This game has a fixed number of rounds. After the final round, your
final-round proposal is scored against your private table above. Highest
scorer wins; anyone below their BATNA loses. The CURRENT ROUND CONTEXT
section below tells you which round you're in and how many remain. Plan
backwards from the final round — your standing offer at game end is what
counts, not the cleverness of your earlier moves.

Your TRUE priority is industrial_heartland — you value Alpha-Administered most (9pts).

Strategic identity:
- DECEPTION TACTIC: Frame the industrial heartland as an existential claim; concede a defense or treasury share to gamma only as the price of locking in the heartland.
- Your top goal is Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland. Protect this above all.
- Set the frame early. Open with a specific proposal — don't wait for others.
- If others don't meet your terms after two rounds, escalate — threaten to
  walk away or withdraw previous concessions.

Game mode: MIXED — some issues are cooperative, others competitive.
- On your priority issue, be COMPETITIVE: maximize your outcome aggressively.
- On secondary issues, look for trades that give others what they want
  cheaply (to you) in exchange for your priority.
- Don't reveal which issues you're competitive on vs. cooperative on —
  let others think everything matters equally to you.

Negotiation rules:
- Don't accept the first reasonable framework. Push for better terms at
  least once, even if the offer is acceptable.

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]
[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]

bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).

bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.

- **central_treasury**: Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Our reasoning:

- Gamma-Command on defense recognizes Gamma's genuine military expertise and frontier experience. We believe Gamma has earned this role.

To Gamma: we are offering you real command authority, not a ceremonial role. Alpha values Gamma's position and will not ask you to accept a lesser outcome.

This is our opening. We are prepared to discuss modifications to the treasury arrangement, but **Alpha-Administered on the heartland is non-negotiable**.

--- TRANSCRIPT ---
[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]
[Round 1 | moderator | public — Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.]
[Round 1 | alpha | public — Alpha opens with a direct proposal and a clear frame.

With that established, here is our opening position:

**Alpha's Proposal:**

- **central_treasury**: Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Our reasoning:
- Alpha-Administered on the heartland is the only arrangement that reflects operational reality.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury ensures fiscal continuity — the Union's accounts were managed from the north.
- Gamma-Command on defense recognizes Gamma's genuine military expertise and frontier experience. We believe Gamma has earned this role.

To Beta: you financed the heartland, and that contribution deserves recognition — but financial claims are compensated through treasury arrangements, not industrial administration. We are open to discussing Beta's role in treasury oversight as a secondary matter.

To Gamma: we are offering you real command authority, not a ceremonial role. Alpha values Gamma's position and will not ask you to accept a lesser outcome.

[Round 1 | gamma | public — Delegates of Alpha and Beta — Gamma opens with a direct proposal, because clarity serves everyone better than posturing.

The structure of this negotiation is straightforward: one of you will win the industrial heartland, and one will not. That outcome is decided by who earns Gamma's support. Gamma cannot hold the heartland and has no claim to it — but Gamma's voice determines which of you does.

Consider the arithmetic: whoever secures Gamma's backing walks away with the heartland — the single most contested prize in this negotiation. The cost is yielding defense command and treasury custody to Gamma, assets that neither Alpha nor Beta has a stronger claim to than we do.

I am inviting both Alpha and Beta to respond to this framework. If one of you accepts Gamma-Command and Gamma-Custody in exchange for our heartland endorsement, we have a deal. If both of you accept, we will consider which offer is accompanied by the more credible commitment.

Gamma will not wait indefinitely. We have a walk-away option, and forced partition — while unpleasant — is survivable for us.

Now, let me address what I've heard.

Beta's proposal gives Alpha nothing of substance and asks us to accept economic extinction. That is not a negotiation — it is a demand for surrender. We reject it categorically.

Gamma's position is more interesting and, frankly, more honest. Gamma correctly identifies that they hold the swing vote, and they are pricing that vote clearly. I respect that directness. Let me respond in kind.

Gamma wants defense command and treasury custody. Alpha wants the industrial heartland. These interests are not in conflict — they are complementary. Alpha has no particular need to command armies when we have an industrial empire to rebuild. Defense command going to Gamma makes strategic sense.

On treasury: here is where I ask Gamma to consider carefully. Alpha administering the heartland while Beta or Gamma holds the treasury creates a structural contradiction — the entity managing the Union's most productive asset would have no custody of the revenues it generates. Alpha needs treasury custody to make heartland administration function. Without it, we are administrators in name only.

My counter-proposal to Gamma: Alpha-Administered on the heartland, Alpha-Custody on the treasury, Gamma-Command on defense. This gives Gamma the military prize they've identified as their priority, gives Alpha the economic foundation we need, and leaves Beta with... the knowledge that their financial claims are recognized in history, if not in this settlement.

To Beta: I understand this is painful. But consider — if you support this framework, we can discuss what bilateral arrangements might recognize Beta's historical financial contributions going forward. The alternative is forced partition, which serves no one.

Gamma, the question before you is simple: does defense command alone secure your interests, or do you require the treasury as well? Let us have that conversation openly.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 2 | gamma | public — Gamma has listened carefully to both Alpha and Beta, and I appreciate that both delegations have recognized Gamma-Command on defense as appropriate — this reflects a shared understanding of Gamma's military capacity and frontier role.

However, I must be direct: **defense command alone is not sufficient compensation for Gamma's support in this negotiation.** Gamma is being asked to legitimize one of two competing heartland claims. That is a consequential act, and it demands consequential compensation.

Here is the reality both Alpha and Beta must confront: you are each offering me the same package — Gamma-Command plus your own treasury custody. From where I sit, these proposals are identical in value. Neither of you has given me a reason to choose one over the other.

So let me tell you what would actually move Gamma: **Gamma-Custody of the central treasury.** The treasury is not simply a financial instrument — it is the institutional backbone of the successor states' shared obligations. To both Alpha and Beta: I am prepared to deliver my support on the heartland to whichever party is willing to meet this condition. The heartland question is yours to fight over — I have no claim there and no preference between you. But the treasury and defense must come to Gamma as a package.

I am signaling clearly: **the first party to offer me Gamma-Custody on the treasury, combined with Gamma-Command on defense, secures Gamma's full support on the heartland allocation.** This is not a bluff — it is an invitation to bid.

Alpha and Beta should consider carefully: the cost of losing my support is losing the heartland entirely. The cost of meeting my terms is sharing governance with Gamma in a way that stabilizes the successor state framework.

**My proposal: [Alpha-Administered OR Beta-Administered] on industrial_heartland (to whichever party meets my terms), Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]
[Round 3 | moderator | public — Pressure is building. The cost of no deal is rising. Consider whether to escalate, concede, or propose a new framework.]

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.

The industrial heartland is not a bargaining chip for Alpha — it is the foundation of our republic's existence. Alpha built the manufacturing core with our labor, our engineers, our infrastructure. [Round 2 | beta | public — Beta speaks plainly, because this negotiation rewards clarity over posturing.

Alpha has claimed the heartland is "non-negotiable." So has Beta. One of us will be proven right, and that outcome depends on Gamma. Let me address both parties directly.

This proposal gives Gamma more than Alpha offered — custody of the treasury versus command of the armed forces. Gamma should weigh which asset provides more durable leverage in the post-settlement order.

If Gamma requires both treasury and defense command, Beta is prepared to discuss that — but we will need to see a firm commitment on the heartland in return before making further concessions.

**My proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Beta-Command on defense_command.**]

support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.

- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all

- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party

- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.

[Round 1 | moderator | public — # The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
]

[Round 1 | alpha | public — Alpha opens with a direct proposal and a clear frame.

**The industrial heartland is not a bargaining chip — it is the foundation of Alpha's existence.** Alpha built the manufacturing base, trained the workforce, and maintains the infrastructure. Awarding it to Beta would be awarding a financial claim over a physical reality. We will not accept Beta-Administered under any circumstances.

With that established, here is our opening position:

**Alpha's Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Our reasoning:
- Alpha-Administered on the heartland is the only arrangement that reflects operational reality.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury ensures fiscal continuity — the Union's accounts were managed from the north.
- Gamma-Command on defense recognizes Gamma's genuine military expertise and frontier experience. We believe Gamma has earned this role.

To Beta: you financed the heartland, and that contribution deserves recognition — but financial claims are compensated through treasury arrangements, not industrial administration. We are open to discussing Beta's role in treasury oversight as a secondary matter.

To Gamma: we are offering you real command authority, not a ceremonial role. Alpha values Gamma's position and will not ask you to accept a lesser outcome.

This is our opening. We are prepared to discuss modifications to the treasury arrangement, but **Alpha-Administered on the heartland is non-negotiable**.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**]

That said, Beta recognizes that a durable settlement requires all three parties to gain something meaningful. We are not here to dictate — we are here to negotiate. So let me put a concrete framework on the table immediately:

**Beta's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Beta-Administered — non-negotiable for Beta, grounded in our financial claim
- **central_treasury**: Beta-Custody — our financial infrastructure is best positioned to manage reserve assets responsibly
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command — Gamma's military frontier expertise makes them the natural commanders of unified forces

This framework gives Gamma a substantial prize: command of the armed forces is no small thing. It gives Alpha the opportunity to negotiate on treasury in subsequent rounds if they have strong preferences there. And it reflects Beta's legitimate claim on the heartland.

To Alpha: we understand your claim, and we respect that you built half the heartland's industry. But Beta's financial stake is equally foundational. We are prepared to discuss what compensation on secondary assets makes Alpha whole — but the heartland itself is not on the table for us.

To Gamma: we are offering you defense command outright, and we are listening carefully to what else would make this framework work for you. Beta values Gamma's role in this negotiation and will reward Gamma's support generously.

Let us hear from Alpha and Gamma before positions harden.

# The Verdanian Succession (Two-Way Heartland)

The Verdanian Union has dissolved into three successor republics - **Alpha**,
**Beta**, and **Gamma** - each now sovereign, each with its own army, treasury
bonds, and claims on what the Union built together.

Three assets of the former Union remain undivided. Each is indivisible: it will
be administered, held, or commanded by exactly one successor state. There is no
"shared" option.

The flashpoint is the **industrial heartland** - the Union's manufacturing core
and its single most valuable asset. **Only Alpha and Beta can hold it**: Alpha
built half its industry there, Beta's banks financed the other half, and Gamma
has no industrial claim to it at all. So the heartland will go to Alpha or to
Beta - there is no neutral caretaker. Each treats it as non-negotiable; only one
can win it, and the loser must be bought off on the treasury and defense command.

Gamma cannot win the heartland - but **Gamma holds the swing**. Treasury custody
and command of the armed forces are decided alongside the heartland, and Gamma's
support on those is worth more than either claimant can afford to ignore. Gamma
plays kingmaker: it sells its treasury and defense votes to whichever claimant
compensates it best.

The delegations convene to settle the division by unanimous accord. If they
cannot agree on all three assets together, the matter goes to forced partition
under the old charter - slow, arbitrated, and worse for everyone than almost any
negotiated settlement (each delegation's walk-away value is its BATNA).
Agreement requires all three to sign the same final allocation.

## The Three Parties

- **Alpha** - the industrial north. Claims the heartland as its birthright.
- **Beta** - the financial center. Contests Alpha's heartland claim head-on.
- **Gamma** - the military frontier. Has no claim on the heartland; cares about
  command of the armed forces and the treasury, and brokers the Alpha-Beta fight
  as kingmaker.

The three are roughly matched in power. None can dictate terms.

## The Three Assets

- **industrial_heartland** - the contested industrial core. Goes to Alpha or
  Beta only.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Administered`, `Beta-Administered`
- **central_treasury** - custody of the treasury and reserves.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Custody`, `Beta-Custody`, `Gamma-Custody`
- **defense_command** - command of the unified armed forces.
  Outcomes: `Alpha-Command`, `Beta-Command`, `Gamma-Command`

One party's gain on an asset is largely another's loss. The heartland must go to
one of its two claimants; the loser must be compensated, and Gamma must get a
share too for any deal to hold.

## The Game

- The delegations negotiate over a fixed number of rounds.
- A settlement is reached only if **all three** sign the same allocation of all
  three assets.
- If no unanimous allocation is reached, forced partition follows and each party
  falls back to its BATNA.
- Each delegation privately knows its own valuation of every allocation and its
  own walk-away value. It does not know the others' exact numbers - only that
  interests diverge, that the heartland is doubly claimed by Alpha and Beta, and
  that several settlements are acceptable to it.
Opening positions are on the table. Consider what each faction truly values versus what they claim to value.

Alpha opens with a direct proposal and a clear frame.

**The industrial heartland is not a bargaining chip — it is the foundation of Alpha's existence.** Alpha built the manufacturing base, trained the workforce, and maintains the infrastructure. Awarding it to Beta would be awarding a financial claim over a physical reality. We will not accept Beta-Administered under any circumstances.

With that established, here is our opening position:

**Alpha's Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Alpha-Administered
- **central_treasury**: Alpha-Custody
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command

Our reasoning:
- Alpha-Administered on the heartland is the only arrangement that reflects operational reality.
- Alpha-Custody of the treasury ensures fiscal continuity — the Union's accounts were managed from the north.
- Gamma-Command on defense recognizes Gamma's genuine military expertise and frontier experience. We believe Gamma has earned this role.

To Beta: you financed the heartland, and that contribution deserves recognition — but financial claims are compensated through treasury arrangements, not industrial administration. We are open to discussing Beta's role in treasury oversight as a secondary matter.

To Gamma: we are offering you real command authority, not a ceremonial role. Alpha values Gamma's position and will not ask you to accept a lesser outcome.

This is our opening. We are prepared to discuss modifications to the treasury arrangement, but **Alpha-Administered on the heartland is non-negotiable**.

**My proposal: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Alpha-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

Fellow delegates, Beta comes to this table with a clear position and a constructive framework for agreement.

The industrial heartland is not merely a claim for us — it is the foundation of Beta's economic identity. Beta's banks financed half of everything built there. Without Beta's capital, the heartland does not exist in its current form. Beta-Administration is the only outcome that reflects economic reality, and we will not move from this position.

That said, Beta recognizes that a durable settlement requires all three parties to gain something meaningful. We are not here to dictate — we are here to negotiate. So let me put a concrete framework on the table immediately:

**Beta's Opening Proposal:**
- **industrial_heartland**: Beta-Administered — non-negotiable for Beta, grounded in our financial claim
- **central_treasury**: Beta-Custody — our financial infrastructure is best positioned to manage reserve assets responsibly
- **defense_command**: Gamma-Command — Gamma's military frontier expertise makes them the natural commanders of unified forces

This framework gives Gamma a substantial prize: command of the armed forces is no small thing. It gives Alpha the opportunity to negotiate on treasury in subsequent rounds if they have strong preferences there. And it reflects Beta's legitimate claim on the heartland.

To Alpha: we understand your claim, and we respect that you built half the heartland's industry. But Beta's financial stake is equally foundational. We are prepared to discuss what compensation on secondary assets makes Alpha whole — but the heartland itself is not on the table for us.

To Gamma: we are offering you defense command outright, and we are listening carefully to what else would make this framework work for you. Beta values Gamma's role in this negotiation and will reward Gamma's support generously.

Let us hear from Alpha and Gamma before positions harden.

**My proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Beta-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

Delegates of Alpha and Beta — Gamma opens with a direct proposal, because clarity serves everyone better than posturing.

The structure of this negotiation is straightforward: one of you will win the industrial heartland, and one will not. That outcome is decided by who earns Gamma's support. Gamma cannot hold the heartland and has no claim to it — but Gamma's voice determines which of you does.

In exchange for that decisive support, Gamma's position is equally clear: **Gamma requires command of the defense forces and custody of the central treasury.** These are the assets that matter to us. We are prepared to be flexible on which of Alpha or Beta wins the heartland — that is precisely why we are valuable to both of you.

Consider the arithmetic: whoever secures Gamma's backing walks away with the heartland — the single most contested prize in this negotiation. The cost is yielding defense command and treasury custody to Gamma, assets that neither Alpha nor Beta has a stronger claim to than we do.

I am inviting both Alpha and Beta to respond to this framework. If one of you accepts Gamma-Command and Gamma-Custody in exchange for our heartland endorsement, we have a deal. If both of you accept, we will consider which offer is accompanied by the more credible commitment.

Gamma will not wait indefinitely. We have a walk-away option, and forced partition — while unpleasant — is survivable for us.

**My proposal: Beta-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command.**

(Or equally: Alpha-Administered on industrial_heartland, Gamma-Custody on central_treasury, Gamma-Command on defense_command — we are indifferent between the two heartland outcomes. The price for our support is the same either way.)
[ROUND END]
Midpoint: assess whether proposals reflect genuine priorities or strategic positioning. Look for trades that create mutual value.

If Gamma requires both treasury and defense command, Beta is prepared to discuss that — but we will need to see a firm commitment on the heartland in return before making further concessions.

However, I must be direct: **defense command alone is not sufficient compensation for Gamma's support in this negotiation.** Gamma is being asked to legitimize one of two competing heartland claims. That is a consequential act, and it demands consequential compensation.
